title: Display Cable Replacement (Internal E3 Fix)
description: Full internal wiring replacement. Required when the E3 error persists after a screen swap, indicating a cable break inside the steerer or frame.

steps.title: Power Down & Open Deck
steps.desc: Ensure the scooter is off. Remove the deck cover and front mudguard to expose the controller and internal wiring.

steps.title: Release Lower Brake Cable
steps.desc: Loosen the pinch bolt at the front wheel. Slide the brake cable out from the pinch bolt cover through the guide hole.

steps.title: Release Upper Brake Lever
steps.desc: Pull the brake lever, align the slots in the adjustment nuts, and slide the brake cable out of the lever assembly.
steps.tip: Aligning the slots allows the silver wire to pop right out.

steps.title: Disconnect Display from Handlebar
steps.desc: Pop the handlebar out of the top of the steerer tube and unplug the display cable connector.

steps.title: Remove Top Cap Grommet
steps.desc: Fold the steerer tube down. Use a flathead screwdriver to pry out the grommet. Use a knife to cut the grommet for removal if it's stuck.
steps.warning: Be careful not to scratch the paint on the steerer tube.

steps.title: Extract Brake Cable
steps.desc: Pull the brake cable completely out through the top cap hole to clear the path for the electronic cable.

steps.title: Expose Frame Grommet
steps.desc: Remove the two frame grommet cover bolts located near the folding mechanism.

steps.title: Unplug from Controller
steps.desc: Unplug the display cable from the microcontroller (ESC) inside the battery compartment.

steps.title: Remove Old Cable
steps.desc: Pull the display cable out through the frame grommet hole, then pull the head through the frame inlet port.

steps.title: Feed New Display Cable
steps.desc: Feed the new cable into the top cap hole, through the frame inlet, and finally into the frame grommet hole.
steps.tip: Straighten the cable head as much as possible to help it pass through the tight inlet port.

steps.title: Secure Frame Connections
steps.desc: Connect the cable to the ESC. Tuck cables neatly into the frame grommet and secure the cover with bolts.

steps.title: Reinstall Handlebar
steps.desc: Feed the cable through the steerer tube, reconnect it to the display, and reinsert the handlebar.

steps.title: Route & Lock Brake Cable
steps.desc: Feed the brake cable back through the top cap. Reattach it to the brake lever and rotate the nuts to lock it.

steps.title: Brake Tensioning
steps.desc: Pull the cable tight at the wheel using pliers while tightening the pinch bolt. Test the lever feel.
steps.warning: Ensure the brake lever feels firm before riding. Do not over-tighten the pinch bolt or you may snap the cable.
